name: Chicken Cordon Bleu in Pastry
recipeIngredient: 4 skinless, boneless chicken breast halves (about 1 pound)
1/2 package Pepperidge Farm® frozen puff pastry (1 sheet, 17 sheets, 1/4-ounce size)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium skillet over medium-high heat, melt the margarine.
2. Season the chicken with salt and pepper if desired.
3. Add the chicken to the skillet and cook until browned. Remove the chicken from the skillet and cover. Refrigerate for at least 15 minutes.
4. Thaw the puff pastry sheet at room temperature for 30 minutes.
5. Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C).
6. Mix the egg and water in a small bowl and set aside.
7. Unfold the puff pastry on a lightly floured surface. Roll into a 14-inch square and cut into 4 squares (7 inches each).
8. Spread 1 teaspoon of mustard on each square.
9. Place 1 slice of ham, 1 slice of cheese, and 1 chicken breast half onto each square.
10. Brush the edges of the squares with the egg mixture.
11. Fold the corners to the center over the chicken and seal the edges.
12. Place the seam-side down on a shallow-sided baking sheet.
13. Brush the tops with the remaining egg mixture.
14. Bake for 25 minutes or until golden brown.
